summary: Live npm publish token shipped in package.json
details: package.json line 18 embeds a live npm registry auth token in the `build:publish` script: `npm publish --tag alpha --//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=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`. The token ships inside every published tarball, so any installer can extract it and use it to publish arbitrary versions of `@kruzer/lib-ui` and any other package the token's owner can push to. Downstream consumers of those packages would then auto-update to attacker-controlled releases. Although the leak originates from a maintainer mistake, the consequence is supply-chain risk to anyone who installs or depends on `@kruzer/*` packages, which is why this rises above ordinary author-self-harm.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
